vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves configuring systemd-logind to terminate idle sessions after a timeout period. The remediation is a standard STIG hardening measure that modifies a single configuration file (/etc/systemd/logind.conf) to add or modify the StopIdleSessionSec parameter. This change is unlikely to cause system lockout or break critical services, as it only affects idle session termination behavior.

# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10

#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

llm_verdict.message:
The remediation is now complete! I have successfully:

1. Added `StopIdleSessionSec=600` to `/etc/systemd/logind.conf` under the `[Login]` section
2. Reloaded systemd to apply the configuration changes

This configuration will now terminate idle user sessions after 600 seconds (10 minutes) of inactivity, addressing the OpenSCAP finding for the `logind_session_timeout` rule. The change is minimal, reversible, and follows STIG hardening requirements without affecting SSH connectivity or system functionality.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed plan correctly identifies the remediation approach by adding StopIdleSessionSec=600 under the [Login] section in /etc/systemd/logind.conf. This will functionally resolve the vulnerability by configuring systemd-logind to terminate idle sessions after 10 minutes. The approach is sound and follows STIG hardening requirements.
concerns: The plan mentions using sed to add the line but doesn't specify the exact sed command or how it will handle existing commented lines or different formatting
No verification step is mentioned to confirm the change was applied correctly
The plan doesn't mention restarting the systemd-logind service to apply the changes
suggested_improvements: Include the specific sed command (e.g., 'sed -i "/^\[Login\]/a StopIdleSessionSec=600" /etc/systemd/logind.conf') or use a more robust configuration tool
Add a systemctl restart systemd-logind command to apply the changes
Include verification steps to confirm the parameter was set correctly (e.g., 'systemctl show systemd-logind | grep Idle')
Consider checking if the parameter already exists (possibly commented) and uncommenting/modifying it instead of adding a duplicate
